summary: EduMap: AI-ML Powered Global Admission Navigator — a web platform that simplifies college selection for students in India and abroad. Features personalized ML-based college recommendations, real-time college comparison, Google Maps integration for location insights, structured admission guidelines, and an NLP-based AI chatbot for query support. Centralizes data on fees, rankings, eligibility, and admission requirements.
architectural_notes: Frontend: Web app for student input, recommendations, comparisons, and chatbot interaction
Backend: Server layer handling ML prediction, NLP processing, and database communication
ML Module: Content-based filtering (profile-to-college matching) + K-Means clustering
NLP Chatbot: Intent recognition and entity extraction for admission-related queries
Database: Stores college data, student profiles, parameters, and metadata
Google Maps API: Geolocation coordinates and visual map data for college locations
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- EduMap logo mark — a globe with a graduation cap and map pin overlay in teal and amber tones (edumap-logo.dim_256x256.png)
- Hero section background illustration — abstract world map with glowing connection lines and university building silhouettes in deep teal gradient (hero-bg.dim_1440x600.png)
- Graduation student illustration — diverse students celebrating with documents and a globe, flat illustration style in teal and amber (students-hero.dim_600x400.png)

existing_features.title: Apply a cohesive visual theme across the entire application: use a clean academic-tech aesthetic with deep teal and amber accent colors, modern sans-serif typography, card-based layouts, and subtle gradient hero sections. The design should feel trustworthy, international, and accessible for student users.

Create a Landing / Home page that introduces EduMap with a hero section (headline, subheadline, call-to-action button), a feature highlights section showcasing the six core modules (Profile, Recommendations, Comparison, Maps, Guidelines, Chatbot), and a brief 'How It Works' step-by-step section.

Build a Student Profile form page where users can input their academic details: stream (Science/Commerce/Arts), percentage/marks, budget range (min/max in INR/USD), areas of interest (multi-select: Engineering, Medicine, Management, Law, Arts, etc.), preferred region (India / USA / UK / Canada / Australia / Other), and preferred college type (Government / Private / Deemed). Store submitted profiles in the backend.

Build a Recommendations page that takes the stored student profile, matches it against college records using a content-based scoring algorithm (weighing stream match, budget fit, marks eligibility, and region preference), and displays a ranked list of recommended colleges. Each result card must show college name, location, fees, ranking, match score, and a 'View Details' action.

Build a College Comparison page where users can select 2–4 colleges from a searchable dropdown and view a side-by-side comparison table. The table must compare: fees, national ranking, global ranking, eligibility criteria, available courses, college type, location, and admission deadlines. Include a 'Best Value' highlight for the most affordable eligible option.

Build a College Map page that displays college locations on an interactive world map using a visual map component (Leaflet.js or equivalent). Each college is shown as a pin/marker. Clicking a marker opens a popup with college name, city, fees, and ranking. Include filter controls (by country, stream) to show/hide pins.

Build an Admission Guidelines page that provides structured, step-by-step admission guidance organized by destination (India / USA / UK / Canada / Australia). For each destination, show: required documents checklist, typical application process steps, key exam requirements (e.g., JEE, SAT, IELTS, TOEFL, GRE), and general timeline/deadlines. Use an accordion or tabbed layout.

Build an AI Chatbot widget that appears as a floating chat button on all pages. When opened, it renders a chat interface where users can type questions. The chatbot backend should match user queries against a predefined knowledge base (colleges, fees, courses, admission requirements, documents) using keyword/intent matching and return relevant answers. Support at least the following intents: fee inquiry, eligibility inquiry, document requirements, application deadline, course availability, and ranking inquiry.

Add a College Detail page accessible from recommendation cards and comparison results. The page must display full college information: name, description, location, all available courses, fees breakdown, eligibility criteria, required documents, application steps, ranking details, and a mini map pin showing its location.
Add a responsive top navigation bar with the EduMap logo, links to Home, Profile, Recommendations, Compare, Map, Guidelines, and a Chatbot toggle. Include a mobile hamburger menu for small screens.

new_feature_requests.summary: User Input & Profile Module — collect student marks, stream, budget, interests, preferred region
ML Recommendation Module — content-based filtering and K-Means clustering for personalized college suggestions
College Comparison Module — real-time comparison by fees, rankings, eligibility, admission requirements
Google Maps Integration — display college locations and geographic insights
Admission Guidelines Module — structured info on documents, procedures, and application steps
NLP AI Chatbot Module — intent recognition and entity extraction to answer queries on colleges, fees, courses, and admissions

Implement a backend data model to store college records with the following fields: name, country, city, stream, courses offered, annual fees (INR/USD), ranking (national and global), eligibility criteria (minimum marks/scores), admission requirements (list of documents), application deadline, college type (Government/Private), and geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude).
